data.title: Lexicon outlines 2026 milestones as SONATA-HCM enrollment surpasses 50% and cash position is strengthened
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Lexicon Pharmaceuticals (LXRX) Q4 2025

Management View

  • Michael Exton, CEO, highlighted, "Lexicon is advancing 3 very strong novel late-stage programs in the therapeutic areas of cardiometabolic disease and chronic pain." He emphasized progress in sotagliflozin development for hypertrophic cardiomyopathy (HCM), plans for an NDA submission in type 1 diabetes, and a partnership with Viatris for markets outside the U.S. and Europe. The company is also advancing LX9851 in obesity with Novo Nordisk and pilavapadin for diabetic peripheral neuropathic pain (DPNP), for which it seeks a development partner. Exton stated, "We've also been diligent about driving operational excellence as well as improving our financial position and cost structure to sustainably support our core programs going forward."
  • Exton further noted, "The SONATA-HCM Phase III trial of SOTA for obstructive and nonobstructive HCM is enrolling well, and we expect to complete enrollment in the middle of this year." He outlined expectations to resubmit the NDA for Zynquista in 2026 with potential approval later that year, and underscored the bolstered financial position after a recent capital raise and a Novo Nordisk milestone payment.
  • CFO Scott Coiante reported, "Total revenues were $5.5 million and $49.8 million for the quarter and year ended December 31, 2025, respectively." He added, "Research and development expenses for the fourth quarter of 2025 decreased to $11.3 million from $26.7 million in 2024."
  • Coiante elaborated, "We also meaningfully improved our cost structure for 2025 with operating expenses down $129.5 million for 2025 as compared to '24 reflecting our strategic repositioning in late 2024 and substantially reduced marketing and promotional spend for INPEFA in 2025."

Outlook

  • Coiante provided 2026 guidance: "We expect total operating expenses to be between $100 million and $110 million. R&D expenses are expected to be between $63 million and $68 million and do not include costs associated with Phase III pivotal studies of pilavapadin as our goal would be to move this asset forward with a development partner. SG&A expenses...are expected to range between $37 million and $42 million."
  • Management expects to complete SONATA-HCM enrollment by mid-2026 and resubmit the NDA for Zynquista in 2026, with a potential approval before year-end.

Financial Results

  • Fourth quarter 2025 revenues were $5.5 million, including $4.3 million from the Novo Nordisk agreement and $1.1 million in net sales of INPEFA. Full year revenues totaled $49.8 million, with $45 million from Novo Nordisk and $4.6 million in INPEFA net sales.
  • Net loss for Q4 2025 was ($15.5 million) or ($0.04) per share, improving from ($33.8 million) or ($0.09) per share in Q4 2024. Full year net loss was ($50.3 million) or ($0.14) per share, compared to ($200.4 million) or ($0.63) per share the prior year.
  • As of December 31, 2025, cash, investments, and restricted cash stood at $125.2 million, with the company subsequently raising more than $100 million after year-end.
  • Operating expenses were reduced by $39 million for the fourth quarter of 2025 compared to the prior year and by $129.5 million for the full year, reflecting strategic repositioning and reduced marketing spend.
  • Total debt was reduced by approximately $46.3 million in 2025.

Q&A

  • Matthew Barcus, Jefferies: Asked about STENO1 study data sufficiency for Zynquista approval and review timelines. Exton responded, "We're expecting a 6-month review here. So reemphasizing that the data that we're seeing, we expect a submission this year and as well an approval before the end of 2026." Granowitz added details on DKA rates, confirming alignment with FDA requirements.
  • Yigal Nochomovitz, Citigroup: Inquired about pilavapadin partnership progress and Novo's plans for LX9851. Exton indicated ongoing dialogue with partners and highlighted increased specificity in discussions following regulatory feedback. Regarding LX9851, Exton emphasized Novo's strategic pivot toward oral obesity treatments, while Granowitz described the complementary mechanism to semaglutide.
  • Joseph Pantginis, H.C. Wainwright: Questioned pilavapadin development flexibility and the impact of supporting studies for SOTA in HCM. Exton clarified the company is not planning to start Phase III on its own, prioritizing investment in T1D and HCM. Granowitz explained the supportive nature of the SOTA-CARDIA and SOTA-CROSS studies for future sNDAs.
  • Shannon (Piper Sandler): Asked about SONATA-HCM enrollment pace. Granowitz affirmed enrollment is ahead of projections, with both obstructive and nonobstructive cohorts progressing, and Exton stated confidence in achieving midyear enrollment targets.
  • Byunghyun Ahn, Leerink Partners: Sought clarification on pilavapadin Phase III enrollment criteria. Granowitz noted expanded eligibility due to a renal impairment study and possible changes to the minimum neuropathy duration requirement.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts' questions focused on regulatory timelines, partnership clarity, and trial progress, reflecting a neutral to slightly positive tone and seeking confirmation of catalysts. There was an emphasis on timing and risk mitigation.
  • Management presented a confident and optimistic tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently emphasizing operational discipline and program momentum. Exton stated, "We have the pipeline. We have the team, and we have the momentum, and I'm incredibly excited about what lies ahead."
  • Compared to the prior quarter, management's tone has shifted from cautious progress updates to highlighting tangible achievements and forward momentum, while analysts remained consistently focused on execution risks and milestone deliverability.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance for 2026 operating expenses was narrowed to $100–110 million, from $105–115 million previously. R&D expense expectations shifted lower, while SG&A guidance increased slightly. The company reported a strengthened cash position following additional capital raises, compared to $145 million in cash at Q3 end.
  • Strategic focus continues on advancing late-stage assets in cardiometabolic and pain indications, but the narrative now includes clear timing for SONATA-HCM enrollment completion and NDA resubmission for Zynquista.
  • Analysts' questions remain centered on regulatory and partnership timelines, though with greater emphasis on near-term catalysts and financial flexibility versus prior quarter's focus on strategic reorientation and foundational progress.
  • Management's confidence and communication of operational discipline have grown, with a more robust discussion of financial improvements and capital allocation.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted reliance on successful clinical trial enrollment and regulatory review timelines for key programs, especially SONATA-HCM and Zynquista.
  • The company is dependent on partnership execution for pilavapadin's advancement into Phase III, and delays in finding a suitable partner could affect timelines.
  • Analyst questions surfaced concerns regarding the sufficiency of safety data for Zynquista, the cadence and balance of SONATA enrollment, and the specifics of Phase III design for pilavapadin.
  • Ongoing cost management and operational discipline are being prioritized to mitigate financial risks, with no new concerns about liquidity following the recent capital raise.

Final Takeaway

Lexicon Pharmaceuticals enters 2026 with significant momentum across its core programs, supported by a strengthened financial position and operational discipline. The company expects to complete SONATA-HCM enrollment by midyear, targets NDA resubmission and potential approval for Zynquista within the year, and remains focused on securing a partner for pilavapadin while advancing LX9851 in collaboration with Novo Nordisk. Management expresses high confidence in delivering on these milestones, viewing the coming year as pivotal for potential value creation and strategic progress.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Puranium Energy announces proposed non-brokered private placement
data.description:
• Puranium Energy (CSE: UX) on Thursday said that it has a non-brokered private placement of up to 7,252,781 units at a price of $0.08 per unit for gross proceeds of $580,223. 
  • Each unit will be comprised of one common share and one common share purchase warrant, with each warrant exercisable at $0.125 for a period of thirty-six months from the closing of the offering.
  • All securities issued pursuant to the offering will be subject to a four-month-and-one-day statutory hold period and legended accordingly.
  • No finders fees will be paid in connection with the offering.
  • Proceeds will be used toward general working capital, including about $185,000 to settle creditor obligations.
  • MREYD closed +31.14% at $0.08.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Athabasca Oil GAAP EPS of $0.24, revenue of $301.0M
data.description:
• Athabasca Oil press release (ATHOF): FY GAAP EPS of $0.24.
  • Revenue of $301.0M.
  • Cash Flow: Adjusted Funds Flow of $504 million ($1.01 per share). Cash flow from operating activities of $520 million. Free Cash Flow of $217 million from Athabasca (Thermal Oil) demonstrates the resilience of a quality asset base and clean balance sheet. DEC growth was self-funded separately within its cash flow and balance sheet.
  • Capital Program: $323 million total capital expenditures, consistent with guidance, including $231 million at Leismer to support the progressive growth project and $75 million in Duvernay development.
  • Shareholder Returns: Purchased 39 million shares through the Company’s buyback program for an aggregate $230 million, demonstrating its commitment to return 100% of Free Cash Flow to shareholders in 2025. The Company has now purchased ~$720 million in shares and has reduced its fully diluted share count by 24% since commencing the buyback program in 2023. Following the expiry of its current Normal Course Issuer Bid (“NCIB”) on March 17, 2026 the Company will renew a fourth annual NCIB with the Toronto Stock Exchange.

data.title: Compass Therapeutics GAAP EPS of -$0.09
data.description:
• Compass Therapeutics press release (CMPX): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.09.

  • Net loss for the year ended December 31, 2025, was $66.5 million or $0.42 per common share, compared to $49.4 million or $0.36 per common share for the same period in 2024.

    Research and Development (R&D) Expenses

    R&D expenses were $56.0 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, as compared to $42.3 million for the same period in 2024, an increase of $13.7 million or 32%. The increase was primarily attributable to an increase in manufacturing expenses related to tovecimig of $7.7 million and manufacturing expenses related to CTX-10726 of $5.9 million.

    General and Administrative (G&A) Expenses

    G&A expenses were $16.9 million for the year ended December 31, 2025, as compared to $15.1 million for the same period in 2024, an increase of $1.8 million or 12%. The increase was primarily driven by pre-commercialization expenses of $0.7 million and advisory fees of $0.5 million.

    Cash Position

    As of December 31, 2025, cash and marketable securities were $209 million as compared to $127 million as of December 31, 2024, an increase of $82 million, with an anticipated cash runway into 2028. During 2025, $49 million of net cash was used in operating activities, and this was offset by net proceeds from an underwritten public offering of $129 million.

data.title: South Bow Corporation GAAP EPS of $0.45 beats by $0.05, revenue of $461M misses by $39.57M
data.description:
• South Bow Corporation press release (SOBO): Q4 GAAP EPS of $0.45 beats by $0.05.
  • Revenue of $461M (-5.5% Y/Y) misses by $39.57M.
  • South Bow is reaffirming its 2026 guidance, with the Company's financial outlook underpinned by highly contracted cash flows and strong structural demand for services. Normalized EBITDA is projected to be approximately $1.03 billion, within a range of 2%, with approximately 90% secured through committed arrangements, which carry minimal commodity price or volumetric risk.
  • Normalized EBITDA for the Keystone Pipeline System segment is expected to be approximately $15 million lower in 2026 compared to 2025.
  • Normalized EBITDA for the Marketing segment is expected to be approximately $15 million higher in 2026 compared to 2025, reflecting a recovery from the losses realized in 2025.
  • Normalized EBITDA for the Intra-Alberta & Other segment is expected to increase by approximately $10 million in 2026 relative to 2025, with Blackrod Connection Project cash flows increasing throughout the second half of 2026 and into 2027.
  • Normalized EBITDA for the first quarter of 2026 is expected to be relatively unchanged from fourth-quarter 2025 normalized EBITDA of $252 million.
  • South Bow has placed the Blackrod Connection Project into commercial service and will update its outlook for growth capital expenditures once it sanctions its next development project. The Company continues to expect its net debt-to-normalized EBITDA ratio to decrease modestly through 2026.

data.title: Hasbro prices $400 million public offering of 4.65% notes due 2031
data.description:
• Hasbro (HAS) on Thursday said it priced an underwritten public offering of $400 million in 4.650% notes due 2031, with interest accruing from March 12, 2026, and semi annual payments beginning September 12, 2026.

  • The company expects to receive net proceeds of $397 million after the underwriting discount, which it intends to use for general corporate purposes, including redeeming or repaying outstanding debt.

  • The offering is expected to settle on or about March 12, 2026, subject to customary closing conditions. 

  • The notes are being offered under an effective shelf registration statement filed with the SEC.

  • HAS closed -1.81% at $95.37.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Costco in charts: E-commerce adjusted comparable sales soar 21.7%
Glencore backs Kazakh entrepreneur's purchase of 40% stake in Eurasian Resources - FT
data.description:
Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) has proposed to support a little-known Kazakh entrepreneur's $1.4B bid to buy a 40% stake in miner Eurasian Resources Group, the Financial Times reported Thursday.

Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) reportedly offered an $800M upfront pre-payment in exchange for future shipments of ferrochrome, mainly used in steel production, to help finance Shakhmurat Mutalip's bid, which is close to being finalized, having overtaken a rival bid from Shukhrat Ibragimov, the CEO of Eurasian Resources and son of the company's co-founder.

Mutalip and Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) also are in active discussions over his bid to acquire Glencore's 70% stake in another Kazakh miner, Kazzinc, which FT previously reported could be worth ~$3.5B.

Any transaction would require the approval of all three major shareholders, which has not yet been secured, as well as the Kazakhstan government, which owns a 40% stake.
